cards.back: Av = −hfe·RC/(hie + hfe·RE). Negative = inversion. With RE bypassed: Av = −hfe·RC/hie.
Rin,base = hie + (1+hfe)RE. Total: Rin = R1||R2||Rin,base. RE is 'reflected' (1+hfe) times.
Av ≈ 1 (no inversion), Rin very high, Rout very low ≈ hie/(1+hfe). Buffer/impedance matcher.
Av = hfe·RC/hie (positive, no inversion). Rin ≈ hie/(1+hfe) ≈ 10Ω (very low). Ai ≈ −1.
Actual input = vs × Rin/(Rin+Rs). Source resistance reduces effective input signal.
Replace RC with RC||RL in gain formula. RL in parallel reduces effective output resistance and gain.
Avs = [Rin/(Rin+Rs)] × Av × [RL/(RL+Rout)] if source and load resistance are given.
When ib increases, ic=hfe·ib increases, more current through RC, VRC increases, so VC drops. Output goes opposite to input.

cards.front: Why use CC as second stage (Q4)?
cards.back: CC has very high Rin (doesn't load stage 1), Av≈1 (preserves voltage), very low Rout (drives low-impedance loads).
